breadcrumbs.eitcBreadcrumb: 使用劳动所得税抵免助手

title: 劳动所得税抵免 (EITC) 助手
subTitle: 劳动所得税抵免 (EITC) 确保中低收入工人和家庭获得税收减免。回答几个问题，以确认您是否符合条件。
reqText: 所有标有星号 * 的字段为必填字段。

progressTracker.step1: 步骤 1 的 5: 一般信息

error.skipAheadFilingStatus: 在这一步中，您将被问到您打算如何对您  的税项进行申报。您必须按顺序回答这些问题。
error.skipAheadFilingStatusWith2020: On this step you will be asked about how you plan to file your 2020 taxes. You must answer these questions in order.
error.skipAheadAGI: On this step you will determine your Adjusted Gross Income (AGI) by entering your pre-tax income, any payroll deductions, and Adjustments to Income. You must answer these questions in order.
error.skipAheadQualifyingChildren: On this step you will determine which of your children or other relatives are qualifying children for the purposes of claiming and calculating the Earned Income Tax Credit. You must answer these questions in order.
error.skipAheadResults: Information displayed on this page is dependent upon your answers in previous steps. Please answer all previous steps before advancing to this page.
error.warningHeading: You don't qualify for the Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC)
error.yearError: Error: A tax year is required to continue.
error.citizenError: 错误：必须回答关于公民身份的问题，方可继续操作。
error.citizenDQParagraph1: You must be a U.S. citizen or resident alien to claim the Earned Income Tax Credit. See Publication 519, U.S. Tax Guide for Aliens, to determine if you are a U.S. resident alien for the entire year.
error.citizenDQParagraph2: Note: Select Yes if you are filing a joint return with your spouse for 2021 and either you or your spouse were a U.S. Citizen or Resident alien for the entire year.
error.validSSNError: Error: A response to the social security number question is required to continue.
error.validSSNDQParagraph1: You can't claim the EITC because you don't have a valid social security number issued on or before the due date of your tax return.
error.validSSNDQParagraph2: For more information see Publication 596, Earned Income Tax Credit
error.foreignIncomeError: Error: A response to the Foreign earned income question is required to continue.
error.foreignIncomeDQParagraph1: You can't claim the earned income credit if you file Form 2555, Foreign Earned Income.
error.foreignIncomeDQParagraph2: See Publication 54, Tax Guide for U.S. Citizens and Resident Aliens Abroad, for more information
error.claimedAsDependentError: Error: A response to the dependent question is required to continue.
error.claimedAsDependentDQParagraph1: You don't qualify for the EITC if you are someone else's qualifying child for the EITC.
error.claimedAsDependentDQParagraph2: For more information about whether someone can claim you as a qualifying child for the EITC, see Publication 596, Earned Income Credit, You Cannot Be a Qualifying Child of Another Taxpayer.
error.claimedAsDependentDQParagraph3: You don't qualify for the EITC if you are someone else's dependent unless you have a qualifying child for the EITC.
error.ageError: Error: An age range is required to continue.
error.qualifiedHomelessYouthError: Error: A response to the foster or homeless youth question is required to continue.
error.studentError: Error: A response to the student question is required to continue.
error.fsKnownError: Error: A response to the question is required to continue.
error.filingStatusError: 错误：报税状态必填，方可继续操作。
error.numOfDependentsError: Error: The number of children with a valid Social Security number is required to continue.
error.mfsWarningParagraph1: If you file as married filing separately, you do not qualify for the Earned Income Tax Credit.
error.mfsWarningParagraph2: If you have questions about your filing status, see Publication 501, Dependents, Standard Deduction, and Filing Information.
error.mfsWarningParagraph3: See Publication 596, Earned Income Credit, for information on who qualifies for the EITC.
error.noDependentsAgeWarningParagraph1: You don't qualify for EITC without a qualifying child unless you, or your spouse if filing a joint return, are one of the following ages:
error.noDependentsAgeWarningList1: At least age 19 but not a specified student in 2021, or
error.noDependentsAgeWarningList2: At least age 18 and a qualified former foster youth or qualified homeless youth in 2021.
error.noDependentsMFSWarningParagraph1: You can't claim the EITC  if you are married and not filing a joint return with your spouse unless you lived with a qualifying child for more than half the year and either lived apart from your spouse for the last six months of 2021 or were  legally separated according to your state law under a written separate agreement or a decree of separate maintenance and lived apart from your spouse at the end of 2021.
error.us50PercentError: Error: A response to the main home location question is required to continue.
error.us50PercentWarning: To claim the EITC, your home must have been in the United States for more than half the year. The United States includes the 50 states and the District of Columbia.
error.claimDependent1Error: Error: A response to the question about whether a child who lived with you can be claimed as a dependent is required to continue.
error.claimDependent2Error: Error: A response to the question about whether a child can be claimed as a dependent is required to continue.
error.fileJointReturnError: Error: A response to the joint tax return question is required to continue.
error.entitledJointReturnDeceasedError: Error: A response to the question of filing a joint return with your spouse the year your spouse died is required to continue.
error.intendJointReturnDeceasedError: Error: A response to your joint return question is required to continue.
error.liveWithSpouseError: Error: A response to the question about your spouse's residence is required to continue.
error.maritalStatusError: Error: A marital status is required to continue.
error.mfsLegalDocError: Error: A response to the separation question is required to continue.
error.mfsLegalDocWarning: You can't claim the EITC if you are married and not filing a joint return with your spouse unless you lived with a qualifying child for more than half the year and either lived apart from your spouse for the last six months of 2021 or, if you didn't live apart from your spouse for the last 6 months of 2021, but you did live apart from your spouse at the end of 2021, you were legally separated according to your state law under a written separation agreement or a decree of separate maintenance.
error.mfsLegalDocWarning2: For more information about see Publication 596, Earned Income Tax Credit
error.spouseAgeError: Error: An age range is required to continue.
error.deceasedSpouseAgeError: Error: An age range is required to continue.
error.wasSpouseAliveEndOfYearError: Error: A response to whether your spouse was alive at the end of the year  is required to continue.
error.spouseValidSSNError: Error: A response to the spouse valid Social Security question is required to continue.
error.spouseValidSSNWarning: You can't claim the EITC because your spouse doesn't have a valid social security number issued on or before the due date of your tax return.
error.deceasedSpouseValidSSNError: Error: A response to the spouse valid Social Security question is required to continue.
error.deceasedSpouseValidSSNWarning: You can't claim the EITC if you choose to file a joint return with your deceased spouse because your spouse did not have a valid social security number on the day they passed away.
error.deceasedSpouseValidSSNWarning2: You may qualify for the EITC if you don't file a joint return with your deceased spouse.
error.spouseQualifiedHomelessYouthError: Error: A response to is your spouse foster or homeless youth question is required to continue.
error.deceasedSpouseQualifiedHomelessYouthErro: Error: A response to was your spouse foster or homeless question is required to continue.
error.spouseStudentError: Error: A response to the spouse student question is required to continue.
error.houseUpkeep1Error: Error: A response to the cost of keeping a home question is required to continue.
error.houseUpkeep2Error: Error: A response to the cost of keeping a home question is required to continue.
error.houseUpkeep3Error: Error: A response to the percentage of keeping a home is required to continue.
error.spousesPassingError: Error: A response to your spouse's death date is required to continue.
error.live51PctError: 错误：必须回答关于相关子女这一年是否与您共同生活半年以上的问题，方可继续操作。
error.claimOtherError: Error: A response to the question about whether a child can be claimed on someone else's return is required to continue.
error.claimOtherConfirmError: Error: A response to the question about whether a child will be claimed is required to continue.
error.fileJointError: Error: A response to the question about a child's marital status is required to continue.
error.fileJointConfirmError: Error: A response to the question about why a child is filing a joint return is required to continue.
error.permanentlyDisabledError: Error: A response to the question about a child's disability is required to continue.
error.agiNoIncomeError: 错误：收入必填，方可继续操作。
error.childRelationshipError: Error: A child's relationship to you is  required to continue.
error.isMarriedHoHError: Error: A response to the Are you married question is required to continue.
error.liveApartFromSpouseError: Error: A response to the did you live apart from your spouse question is required to continue.
error.dependentYoungerError: Error: A response to the question about whether a child is younger than you is required to continue.
error.fulltimeStudentError: Error: A response to the question about whether a child was a student is required to continue.
error.dependentSSNError: Error: A response to the child's social security number question is required to continue.
error.childAgeError: Error: An age is required to continue.

toolTip.yearToolTipParagraph1: For wages and other income earned in:
toolTip.citizenToolTipParagraph1: If you are not a U.S. citizen, you're considered an alien.
toolTip.citizenToolTipParagraph2: You're considered a resident alien for the EITC if you have a green card, or you lived in the U.S. for more than a certain number of days.
toolTip.citizenToolTipParagraph3: 有关更多信息和重要例外情况，请参阅 第 519 号刊物，美国外国人税收指南
toolTip.validSSNToolTipParagraph1: You must have a valid Social Security number (SSN) that is issued by the Social Security Administration on or before the due date of your 2021 tax return (including extensions).
toolTip.validSSNToolTipParagraph2: For the EITC, an SSN is a valid SSN unless “Not Valid for Employment” is printed on the social security card and the number was issued solely to allow the recipient of the SSN to apply for or receive a federally funded benefit.  An example of a federally funded benefit is Medicaid.
toolTip.validSSNToolTipParagraph3: We accept a social security card with "Valid for work with DHS authorization" on it only as long as the DHS authorization is still valid.
toolTip.validSSNToolTipParagraph4: For the EITC, we don't accept:
toolTip.validSSNToolTipList2: Social Security number issued solely to allow the recipient of the SSN to apply for or receive a federally funded benefit such as Medicaid
toolTip.qualifiedHomelessYouthToolTipParagraph1: An individual who was a foster youth between the ages of 14-17.
toolTip.qualifiedHomelessYouthToolTipParagraph2: An individual who is homeless and self-supporting.
toolTip.filingStatusToolTipParagraph1: You may claim Head of Household filing status only if you're not married at the end of the year or you lived apart from your spouse for the last six months of the year. You must also have paid more than 50% of the costs of keeping up the home in which you lived with certain relatives or the home of your mother or father.
toolTip.filingStatusToolTipParagraph2: 欲了解更多信息，请参阅 关于第 501 号刊物，受抚养人、标准扣减额和报税信息。
toolTip.filingStatusToolTipParagraph3: To file as a qualifying widow(er) all the following must apply to you:
toolTip.filingStatusToolTipList0: You could have filed a joint return with your spouse for the year your spouse died. It does not matter whether you filed a joint return.
toolTip.filingStatusToolTipList1: Your spouse died in 2019 or 2020 and you did not remarry before the end of 2021.
toolTip.filingStatusToolTipList2: You paid more than half the cost of keeping up a home for the year.
toolTip.filingStatusToolTipList3: You have a child or stepchild whom you can claim as a dependent. This does not include a foster child.
toolTip.filingStatusToolTipList4: This child lived in your home all year, except for temporary absences. Note: There are exceptions for a child who was born or died during the year and for a kidnapped child.
toolTip.filingStatusToolTipParagraph4: For more information see About Publication 501, Dependents, Standard Deduction, and Filing Information.
toolTip.depToolTipParagraph1: Your child is a qualifying child if your child meets four tests. The four tests are:
toolTip.depToolTipParagraph2: To claim EITC with a qualifying child your child must have a valid SSN issued on or before the due date of your 2021 return (including extensions).
toolTip.depToolTipParagraph3: For more information, see Publication 596, Earned Income Credit
toolTip.us50ToolTipHeading: Main Home in the United States
toolTip.us50ToolTipParagraph1: Your home must have been in the United States for more than half of 2021. The United States includes the 50 states and the District of Columbia. It does not include Unites States possessions such as Guam, the Virgin Islands, or Puerto Rico.
toolTip.us50ToolTipHeading2: Military Personnel Stationed Outside the United States
toolTip.us50ToolTipParagraph2: If you're a member of the United States military and you were stationed outside the United States on extended active duty, select Yes.
toolTip.foreignIncomeToolTipParagraph1: This refers to income you earned outside the United States, District of Columbia, or U.S. possessions or territories which you have elected to exclude on a Form 2555, Foreign Earned Income.
toolTip.claimToolTipHeader: You as a Dependent or Qualifying Child
toolTip.claimToolTipParagraph1: In most cases you cannot be a dependent or qualifying child of another taxpayer and claim the EITC.
toolTip.claimToolTipList0: In most cases, if you're under age 19 or a student under age 24 and live with your parents or other relatives in the United States for more than half of the year, select Yes.
toolTip.claimToolTipList1: If you're married and filing a joint return, you can't be claimed as a dependent unless you're only filing a joint return to claim a refund of withheld income taxes or estimated taxes paid.
toolTip.claimToolTipParagraph2: For more information about whether someone can claim you as a dependent, visit Whom May I Claim as a Dependent?
toolTip.claimToolTipParagraph3: For more information about whether someone can claim you as a qualifying child for the EITC, see Publication 596, Earned Income Credit, You Cannot Be a Qualifying Child of Another Taxpayer.
toolTip.claimToolTipParagraph4: We'll ask about children or relatives you might claim later.
toolTip.ageToolTipParagraph1: An individual who was a previous foster youth or homeless between the ages of 14-17.
toolTip.studentToolTipParagraph1: A specified student is an individual who during 2021 carried at least half the normal full-time workload at a higher education institution, for a period of 5 months or greater, and who is not a qualified former foster youth or a qualified homeless youth.
toolTip.studentToolTipParagraph2: For information about whether your educational program or workload meets the qualifications for you to be a specified student, see the Instructions to Form 8863, Education Credits.
toolTip.claimDependentToolTipParagraph1: A step relationship is one formed by a marriage. For the EITC, a relationship formed by a marriage, such as a stepdaughter, stepson, stepbrother, or stepsister, doesn't end if the marriage ends.
toolTip.claimDependentToolTipParagraph2: For more information about whether you can claim someone as a dependent, visit Whom May I Claim as a Dependent?
toolTip.claimDependent2ToolTipParagraph1: A dependent is usually, but not always, a child or other relative who meets a set of criteria. A dependent must be a U.S. citizen, U.S. national or resident alien. Most dependents include:
toolTip.claimDependent2ToolTipList2: Or other relatives who live with you and whose care you are responsible for.
toolTip.claimDependent2ToolTipParagraph2: For more information about whether you can claim someone as a dependent, visit Whom May I Claim as a Dependent?
toolTip.fileJointReturnToolTipParagraph1: You can choose to file one return with your spouse by selecting Married as your filing status. This allows you to combine your income and expenses when figuring your credits, deductions, and tax liability.
toolTip.jointReturnDeceasedHelpTipParagraph1: If your spouse died during 2021, you can choose to file one return with your spouse by selecting Married as your filing status. This allows you to combine your income and expenses when figuring your credits, deductions, and tax liability.
toolTip.liveWithSpouseToolTipParagraph1: A person is considered to have lived with you during periods of time when one or both of you are temporarily absent due to special circumstances such as:
toolTip.liveWithSpouseToolTipParagraph2: It must be reasonable to assume that the absent person will return to the home after the temporary absence.
toolTip.maritalStatusToolTipParagraph1: "Legally separated" for this purpose means legally separated under a decree of divorce or separate maintenance.
toolTip.maritalStatusToolTipParagraph2: Some states allow common law marriages. Check with your state to see if you are considered married under common law.
toolTip.spouseAgeToolTipParagraph1: An individual who was a previous foster youth or homeless between the ages of 14-17.
toolTip.deceasedSpouseAgeToolTipParagraph1: An individual who was a previous foster youth or homeless between the ages of 14-17.
toolTip.spouseSSNToolTipParagraph1: When filing a joint return, you and your spouse must have a valid Social Security number (SSN) that is issued by the Social Security Administration on or before the due date of your 2021 tax return (including extensions).
toolTip.spouseSSNToolTipParagraph2: For the EITC, an SSN is a valid SSN unless “Not Valid for Employment” is printed on the social security card and the number was issued solely to allow the recipient of the SSN to apply for or receive a federally funded benefit.  An example of a federally funded benefit is Medicaid.
toolTip.spouseSSNToolTipParagraph3: We accept a social security card with "Valid for work with DHS authorization" on it only as long as the DHS authorization is still valid.
toolTip.spouseSSNToolTipParagraph4: For the EITC, we don't accept:
toolTip.spouseSSNToolTipList2: Social Security number issued solely to allow the recipient of the SSN to apply for or receive a federally funded benefit such as Medicaid
toolTip.spouseStudentToolTipParagraph1: A specified student is an individual who during 2021 carried at least half the normal full-time workload at a higher education institution, for a period of 5 months or greater, and who is not a qualified former foster youth or a qualified homeless youth.
toolTip.spouseStudentToolTipParagraph2: For information about whether your educational program or workload meets the qualifications for you to be a specified student, see the Instructions to Form 8863, Education Credits.
toolTip.upkeepToolTipParagraph1: If the total amount you paid is more than half the total cost, you meet the requirement of paying more than half the cost of keeping up the home.
toolTip.upkeepToolTipList0: Rent, mortgage interest, real estate taxes, and home insurance
toolTip.upkeepToolTipList3: Some costs paid with public assistance
toolTip.upkeepToolTipList21: Medical treatment, medical insurance payments, and prescription drugs
toolTip.upkeepToolTipList23: Transportation costs like insurance, lease payments, or public transportation
toolTip.upkeepToolTipList24: Rental value of a home you own
toolTip.upkeepToolTipList25: Value of your services or those of a member of your household
toolTip.retirementToolTipParagraph1: Amounts that you may enter here include payroll deductions to the following retirement plans:
toolTip.retirementToolTipList0: Cash or deferred arrangements (section 401(k) or 403(b) plans, but not Roth plans);
toolTip.retirementToolTipList1: Section 457 plans, but not Roth plans;
toolTip.retirementToolTipList2: The Thrift Savings Plan for federal employees, but not Roth plans;
toolTip.retirementToolTipList3: Salary reduction simplified employee pension (SARSEP) plans;
toolTip.cafeteriaPlanToolTipParagraph1: These are pre-tax contributions (payroll deductions) that may include any of the following:
toolTip.cafeteriaPlanToolTipList0: Coverage under accident and health insurance plans, other than long-term care.
toolTip.cafeteriaPlanToolTipList1: Coverage under dependent care assistance plans.
toolTip.cafeteriaPlanToolTipList2: Group-term life insurance on the lives of employees.
toolTip.wagesWithheldToolTipParagraph1: Your employer reported your wages, salaries, tips, and other compensation in box 1 of your Form W-2.
toolTip.wagesWithheldToolTipParagraph2: Include all amounts reported in box 1 of Form W-2 for both you and your spouse if you're filing a joint tax return with your spouse.
toolTip.wagesWithheldToolTipParagraph3: Don't include your Form W-2 pre-tax contributions in the pre-tax contributions section.
toolTip.wagesWithheldToolTipParagraph4: You can also choose to include your nontaxable combat pay in earned income for the EITC. Your employer reported your nontaxable combat pay in box 12 with code Q on your Form W-2.
toolTip.wagesWithheldToolTipParagraph5: 这一选择可能会导致您的 EITC 额上下浮动。欲了解更多信息，请参阅 “第 596 号刊物，劳动所得抵免”中的非应税军饷
toolTip.wagesWithheldToolTipParagraph6: If you took parental leave or were temporarily disabled during the year and your employer paid you, they reported your income on your W-2. Enter that income here.
toolTip.deferredRetirementToolTipParagraph1: Enter any pre-tax contributions (payroll deductions) for the following retirement plans:
toolTip.deferredRetirementToolTipList0: Cash or deferred arrangements (section 401(k) or 403(b) plans, but not Roth plans)
toolTip.deferredRetirementToolTipList1: Section 457 plans, but not Roth plans
toolTip.deferredRetirementToolTipList2: The Thrift Savings Plan for federal employees
toolTip.deferredRetirementToolTipList3: Salary reduction simplified employee pension (SARSEP) plans
toolTip.cafeteriaToolTipParagraph1: A "cafeteria plan" is a collection of benefits offered by your employer where you can choose what you want to participate in.
toolTip.cafeteriaToolTipParagraph2: Enter any pre-tax contributions (payroll deductions) from any of the following:
toolTip.cafeteriaToolTipList0: Coverage under accident and health insurance plans, other than long-term care
toolTip.cafeteriaToolTipList1: Coverage under dependent care assistance plans
toolTip.cafeteriaToolTipList2: Group-term life insurance on the lives of employees
toolTip.healthInsurancePremiumsToolTipParagraph1: Enter any premiums you paid pre-tax for health insurance that you didn't include in the HSA, FSA, or other “cafeteria plan” section.
toolTip.incomeTaxesNotWithheldToolTipHeader: Income Where Federal Taxes Weren't Withheld
toolTip.incomeTaxesNotWithheldToolTipParagraph1: Enter income here if you earn money from a job but income taxes were not withheld. You may or may not have received  a  Form 1099. Work like this includes:
toolTip.incomeTaxesNotWithheldToolTipList0: Driving a car for booked rides or deliveries
toolTip.incomeTaxesNotWithheldToolTipList4: Providing other temporary, on-demand or freelance work
toolTip.incomeTaxesNotWithheldToolTipParagraph2: For more information, visit Manage Taxes for Your Gig Work
toolTip.selfEmploymentGrossToolTipList1: Make money providing on-demand work, services or goods
toolTip.selfEmploymentGrossToolTipList3: Are a minister or member of a religious order
toolTip.selfEmploymentGrossToolTipParagraph2: Your gross income includes all the income you received. We will subtract your expenses later.
toolTip.selfEmploymentGrossToolTipParagraph3: For more information visit Publications and Forms for the Self-Employed
toolTip.selfEmploymentPaidToolTipParagraph1: Self-employment taxes refers to the Medicare and Social Security taxes you paid. Divide by 2 the total amount you paid for 2021. Enter the result here.
toolTip.sepSimpleToolTipHeader: SEP, SIMPLE, and other qualified plans:
toolTip.sepSimpleToolTipParagraph1: If you are self-employed, you calculate your qualified plan compensation by subtracting the following from your net earnings from self-employment:
toolTip.sepSimpleToolTipList0: The deductible portion of your SE tax from your Form 1040 return, page 1, and
toolTip.sepSimpleToolTipList1: The amount of your retirement plan contribution from your Form 1040 return, page 1, on the line for self-employed SEP, SIMPLE, and qualified plans
toolTip.sepSimpleToolTipParagraph2: For more information see Self-Employed Individuals – Calculating Your Own Retirement-Plan Contribution and Deduction.
toolTip.selfEmploymentInsuranceToolTipParagraph1: Enter the amount you paid for health insurance for yourself, your spouse, and your dependents, if you are self-employed.
toolTip.selfEmploymentInsuranceToolTipParagraph2: For more information see Chapter 6 of Publication 535, Business Expenses for eligibility information.
toolTip.selfEmploymentExpensesToolTipParagraph1: Enter all ordinary and necessary expenses paid or incurred during the taxable year in carrying on any trade or business other than the performance of services as an employee.
toolTip.retirementDisabilityToolTipParagraph1: If you retired on disability, we consider the taxable benefits you received under your employer's disability retirement plan earned income for the purposes of the EITC until you reach minimum retirement age. The minimum retirement age is the earliest age at which you could have received a pension or annuity if you weren't disabled.
toolTip.retirementDisabilityToolTipParagraph2: Include temporary or short-term disability in your wages.
toolTip.foreignToolTipParagraph1: If you're a U.S. government employee working outside the United States, that income is not considered foreign earned.
toolTip.foreignToolTipParagraph2: For more information see Form 2555, Foreign Earned Income.
toolTip.foreignToolTipParagraph3: If you're not a government employee and you work at an embassy, we consider that income as foreign income for the EITC.
toolTip.prisonToolTipParagraph1: Pay you received for work you performed while an inmate in a penal institution isn't earned income when figuring the earned income credit. This includes paid work performed while in a work release program or while in a halfway house.
toolTip.prisonToolTipParagraph2: For more information, see Publication 596, Earned Income Credit, Earnings while an inmate.
toolTip.pensionToolTipParagraph1: If you receive retirement benefits in the form of pension or annuity payments from a qualified employer retirement plan, all or some portion of the amounts you receive may be taxable.
toolTip.pensionToolTipList0: You didn't contribute anything or aren't considered to have contributed anything for your pension or annuity
toolTip.pensionToolTipList1: Your employer didn't withhold contributions from your salary, or
toolTip.pensionToolTipList2: You received all your contributions tax-free in prior years
toolTip.pensionToolTipList20: You contributed after-tax dollars (use the General Rule and Simplified Method to calculate)
toolTip.pensionToolTipParagraph4: Additional rules apply if you received payments before age 59 and a half, are a survivor or beneficiary, or have other situations.
toolTip.pensionToolTipParagraph5: For more information visit Tax Topic 410
toolTip.unemploymentInsuranceToolTipParagraph1: For the EITC, include your full unemployment compensation benefits. You find this income on Form 1099-G.
toolTip.unemploymentInsuranceToolTipParagraph2: If your employer's fund provided supplemental benefits, include any amounts reported on Form W-2, box 1.
toolTip.railroadToolTipParagraph1: For the EITC, include your full Social Security benefits. You find this income on Form SSA-1099, box 5.
toolTip.railroadToolTipParagraph2: For more information visit Tax Topic 423
toolTip.railroadToolTipParagraph3: If you received a Form RRB-1099, enter the full amount found in box 5.
toolTip.railroadToolTipParagraph4: For more information see Publication 915, Social Security and Equivalent Railroad Retirement Benefits, Worksheet A, page 4.
toolTip.grantToolTipParagraph1: Enter any amount of taxable scholarship not reported on your Form W-2. This amount includes scholarship or grant money not found on your W-2 if the money was:
toolTip.grantToolTipList0: Used for incidental expenses, such as room and board, travel, and optional equipment
toolTip.grantToolTipList1: Received as payment for teaching, research, or other services required as a condition for receiving the scholarship or fellowship grant
toolTip.grantToolTipParagraph2: Don't include amounts you received for services required by the National Health Service Corps Scholarship Program, the Armed Forces Health Professions Scholarship and Financial Assistance Program, or a comprehensive student work-learning-service program operated by a work college.
toolTip.grantToolTipParagraph3: For more information visit Tax Topic 421
toolTip.investmentIncomeToolTipParagraph1: Investment income for the EITC includes:
toolTip.investmentIncomeToolTipParagraph2: See Publication 550,  Investment Income and Expenses for more information.
toolTip.investmentIncomeToolTipParagraph3: Use Worksheet 1 in Publication 596, Earned Income Credit, to figure your investment income if any of the following statements are true for 2021:
toolTip.investmentIncomeToolTipList10: You are reporting income from the rental of personal property not used in a trade or business.
toolTip.investmentIncomeToolTipList20: You are reporting from Form 8814 (relating to election to report child's interest and dividends).
toolTip.investmentIncomeToolTipList30: You have income or loss from a passive activity.
toolTip.investmentIncomeToolTipList40: You are filing Form 4797 (relating to sales of business property).
toolTip.otherUnearnedToolTipList0: Individual Retirement Account (IRA) distributions (except Roth IRAs)
toolTip.otherUnearnedToolTipList3: Alimony payments you received under a divorce or separation agreement executed before 2020
toolTip.otherUnearnedToolTipList4: Rental real estate, royalties, partnerships, S corporations, trusts, etc.
toolTip.otherUnearnedToolTipList5: Income from rental of personal property if you engaged in the rental for profit but were not in the business of renting such property
toolTip.otherUnearnedToolTipList6: Taxable distributions from a Coverdell education savings account (ESA) and qualified tuition program earnings
toolTip.otherUnearnedToolTipList11: Reimbursements or other amounts received for items deducted in an earlier year, such as medical expenses, real estate taxes, or home mortgage interest
toolTip.otherUnearnedToolTipList20: Alimony payments you received under a divorce or separation agreement entered into after 2019
toolTip.otherUnearnedToolTipList22: Welfare benefits such as Temporary Assistance for Needy Families (TANF), food stamps, section 8 housing allowance
toolTip.otherUnearnedToolTipList24: Nontaxable employee benefits such as education assistance
toolTip.otherUnearnedToolTipList25: Veterans' benefits, including VA rehabilitation payments
toolTip.otherUnearnedToolTipList27: Life insurance proceeds received because of a person's death
toolTip.otherUnearnedToolTipList28: Money or property that was inherited, willed to you, or received as a gift
toolTip.otherUnearnedToolTipParagraph3: See Publication 525, Taxable and Nontaxable Income
toolTip.studentLoanToolTipParagraph1: Enter up to $2,500 of the interest you paid on your qualified student loan. You can find this on your Form 1098-E if you paid more than $600 in interest.
toolTip.studentLoanToolTipParagraph2: The loan must have paid qualified higher education expenses to attend an eligible educational institution. Also, the expenses must have been:
toolTip.studentLoanToolTipList0: For you, your spouse, or a person who was your dependent when you took out the loan;
toolTip.studentLoanToolTipList1: Paid or incurred within a reasonable period of time before or after you took out the loan; and
toolTip.studentLoanToolTipList2: For education provided during an academic period for an eligible student.
toolTip.studentLoanToolTipParagraph3: You can't claim the deduction if you aren't legally obligated to make payments on the loan.
toolTip.studentLoanToolTipParagraph4: For more information, see Publication 970, Tax Benefits for Education.
toolTip.educatorToolTipParagraph1: If you're a teacher, enter up to $250 ($500 if married filing jointly and both you and your spouse are teachers) of unreimbursed expenses such as:
toolTip.educatorToolTipList0: Fees for participation in professional development courses
toolTip.educatorToolTipList2: Computer equipment (including related software and services)
toolTip.educatorToolTipList4: Supplementary materials that you use in the classroom
toolTip.educatorToolTipParagraph2: For more information see IRS Topic 458
toolTip.iraToolTipParagraph1: Enter any IRA contributions you made outside of your payroll contributions.
toolTip.iraToolTipParagraph2: Don't include amounts that you included above.
toolTip.iraToolTipParagraph3: For more information see IRA Deduction Limits
toolTip.hsaToolTipParagraph1: Enter any Health Savings Account (HSA) contributions that you didn't include under HSA, FSA, or other “cafeteria plan” above.
toolTip.hsaToolTipParagraph2: For more information visit About Publication 969, Health Savings Accounts and Other Tax-Favored Health Plans
toolTip.movingToolTipParagraph1: If you are a member of the armed forces on active duty, and the military ordered you to permanently change your duty station, enter any moving expenses the military didn't reimburse you for here. Moving expenses include:
toolTip.movingToolTipList0: Direct travel from your old home to your new one
toolTip.movingToolTipList1: Cost of packing and moving your personal belongings
toolTip.movingToolTipParagraph2: For more information visit Tax Topic 455
toolTip.alimonyToolTipParagraph1: If you made alimony or separate maintenance payments under a court order that was executed before January 1, 2021 and the order didn't specifically state that you can't deduct your payments, enter the amount you paid during 2021 here.
toolTip.alimonyToolTipParagraph2: Don't include amounts you paid for:
toolTip.alimonyToolTipList1: Alimony or separate maintenance made under a court order executed after December 31, 2020
toolTip.alimonyToolTipParagraph3: Also don't include amounts if the court order was modified and states that you can't deduct your payments.
toolTip.alimonyToolTipParagraph4: For more information visit Tax Topic 452
toolTip.earlyWithdrawlPenaltyToolTipHeader: Penalty for Early Withdrawal of Savings
toolTip.earlyWithdrawlPenaltyToolTipParagraph1: If you withdraw funds from a certificate of deposit or other deferred interest account before maturity, you may be charged a penalty. You can find this penalty on your Form 1099-INT in box 2. Enter that amount here.
toolTip.businessToolTipList2: Fee-basis state or local government official
toolTip.businessToolTipParagraph2: You may include expenses that are common and accepted in your field of trade, business, or profession, or expenses for things that are helpful and appropriate for your business.
toolTip.businessToolTipParagraph3: If you don't fit into one of the listed categories, you may not claim adjustments for these expenses.
toolTip.businessToolTipParagraph4: For more information and a list of qualified expenses, see Form 2106, Employee Business Expenses.
toolTip.live51PctToolTipParagraph1: A qualifying child must have the same main home as you for more than half of 2021 and that home must have been in the United States. The United States includes the 50 states and the District of Columbia. It does not include United States possessions such as Guam, the Virgin Islands, or Puerto Rico. For the EITC, the IRS considers U.S. military personnel stationed outside the United States on extended active duty to have a main home in the United States during that period of duty.
toolTip.live51PctToolTipParagraph2020: A qualifying child must have the same main home as you for more than half of 2020 and that home must have been in the United States. The United States includes the 50 states and the District of Columbia. It does not include United States possessions such as Guam, the Virgin Islands, or Puerto Rico. For the EITC, the IRS considers U.S. military personnel stationed outside the United States on extended active duty to have a main home in the United States during that period of duty.
toolTip.live51PctToolTipHeader2: Birth or Death of a Child
toolTip.live51PctToolTipParagraph2: If the child was born or died in 2021, the child must have lived with you for more than half the part of the year he or she was alive.
toolTip.live51PctToolTipParagraph02020: If the child was born or died in 2020, the child must have lived with you for more than half the part of the year he or she was alive.
toolTip.live51PctToolTipParagraph3: For the EITC, a temporary absence counts as time lived with you. Examples include:
toolTip.live51PctToolTipList1: School attendance, vacation, business or military service
toolTip.live51PctToolTipParagraph4: 欲了解更多信息，请参阅 第 596 号刊物，劳动所得抵免，居所测试。
toolTip.claimOtherToolTipHeader: Claimed on Someone Else's Tax Return
toolTip.claimOtherToolTipParagraph1: Only one person can claim each child for the EITC. If more than one person claims the child, then the IRS applies tiebreaker rules.
toolTip.claimOtherToolTipParagraph2: For more information, see Publication 596, Earned Income Credit, Your Qualifying Child Cannot Be Used By More Than One Person.
toolTip.fileJointToolTipParagraph1: You generally can't claim a married person as a qualifying child for the EITC if he or she files a joint return.
toolTip.fileJointToolTipParagraph2: For more information see Publication 596, Earned Income Credit, Joint Return Test.
toolTip.fileJointConfirmToolTipHeader: Joint Return with Another Person Exception
toolTip.fileJointConfirmToolTipParagraph1: You can claim a married person who files a joint return as a qualifying child for the EITC if the child and their spouse file the joint return only to claim a refund of withheld income taxes from their paychecks or estimated taxes they paid. They may not claim any credits such as the EITC on their return.
toolTip.fileJointConfirmToolTipParagraph2: For more information see Publication 596, Earned Income Credit, Joint Return Test.
toolTip.permanentlyDisabledToolTipParagraph1: For the EITC, we consider a child permanently and totally disabled if both of the following are true:
toolTip.permanentlyDisabledToolTipList0: The child can't perform significant duties over a reasonable period of time while working for pay or profit in a competitive work situation for at least the minimum wage because of a physical or mental condition, and
toolTip.permanentlyDisabledToolTipList1: A physician determines the condition has lasted or can be expected to last continuously for at least a year or can lead to death
toolTip.permanentlyDisabledToolTipParagraph2: For more information see Publication 596, Earned Income Credit, Permanently and Totally Disabled.
toolTip.relationshipToolTipParagraph1: A step relationship is one formed by a marriage. For the EITC, a relationship formed by a marriage, such as a stepdaughter, stepson, stepbrother, or stepsister, doesn't end if the marriage ends.
toolTip.relationshipToolTipParagraph2: An adopted child is always treated as your own child. This term includes a child who was lawfully placed with you for legal adoption.
toolTip.relationshipToolTipParagraph3: You can claim a foster child only if the child is placed with you by an authorized placement agency or by court order.
toolTip.relationshipToolTipParagraph4: This term includes a state or local government agency or an Indian tribal government. It also includes a tax-exempt organization licensed by a state or an Indian tribal government.
toolTip.childYoungerToolTipHeader: Child Must be Younger Than You
toolTip.childYoungerToolTipHeaderMFJ: Child Must be Younger Than You or Your Spouse
toolTip.childToolTipPargraph1: To be a qualifying child, the child must be younger than you unless the child is permanently and totally disabled at any time during 2021.
toolTip.childToolTipPargraphMFJ1: To be a qualifying child, the child must be younger than you or your spouse unless the child is permanently and totally disabled at any time during 2021.
toolTip.childToolTipPargraph12020: To be a qualifying child, the child must be younger than you unless the child is permanently and totally disabled at any time during 2020.
toolTip.childToolTipPargraphMFJ2020: To be a qualifying child, the child must be younger than you or your spouse unless the child is permanently and totally disabled at any time during 2020.
toolTip.childToolTipPargraph2: For more information see Publication 596, Earned Income Credit, Age Test.
toolTip.validSSNQCToolTipHeader: Valid Social Security Number for Qualifying Children
toolTip.validSSNQCToolTipParagraph1: The amount of your EITC depends on the number of qualifying children you have with valid SSNs. Each child or relative you claim for purposes of calculating the amount of the EITC must have a valid Social Security number (SSN) that is issued by the Social Security Administration on or before the due date of your tax return (including extensions).
toolTip.validSSNQCToolTipParagraph2: For the EITC, an SSN is a valid SSN unless “Not Valid for Employment” is printed on the social security card and the number was issued solely to allow the recipient of the SSN to apply for or receive a federally funded benefit. An example of a federally funded benefit is Medicaid.
toolTip.validSSNQCToolTipParagraph3: We accept a social security card with "Valid for work with DHS authorization" on it only as long as the DHS authorization is still valid.
toolTip.validSSNQCToolTipParagraph4: For the EITC, we don't accept:
toolTip.validSSNQCToolTipList3: Social Security number issued solely to allow the recipient of the SSN to apply for or receive a federally funded benefit such as Medicaid
toolTip.us51ToolTipHeader1: Main Home in the United States
toolTip.us51ToolTipParagraph1: Your home must have been in the United States for more than half of 2021. The United States includes the 50 states and the District of Columbia. It does not include Unites States possessions such as Guam, the Virgin Islands, or Puerto Rico.
toolTip.us51ToolTipParagraph2020: Your home must have been in the United States for more than half of 2020. The United States includes the 50 states and the District of Columbia. It does not include Unites States possessions such as Guam, the Virgin Islands, or Puerto Rico.
toolTip.us51ToolTipHeader2: Military Personnel Stationed Outside the United States
toolTip.us51ToolTipParagraph2: If you're a member of the United States military and you were stationed outside the United States on extended active duty, select Yes
toolTip.ftStudentToolTipParagraph1: To be considered full-time, the student must have enrolled for the number of hours or courses the school considers to be full-time attendance.
toolTip.ftStudentToolTipParagraph2: Students who work on "co-op" jobs in private industry as a part of a school's regular course of classroom and practical training are considered full-time students.
toolTip.ftStudentToolTipParagraph3: For the EITC, a school is:
toolTip.ftStudentToolTipList02: School offering courses only through the Internet
toolTip.ftStudentToolTipParagraph5: For more information see Publication 596, Earned Income Credit, Student Defined and School Defined.
toolTip.validSSNHeader1: Valid Social Security Number for Qualifying Children
toolTip.validSSNParagraph1: The amount of your EITC depends on the number of qualifying children you have with valid SSNs. Each child or relative you claim for purposes of calculating the amount of the EITC must have a valid Social Security number (SSN) that is issued by the Social Security Administration on or before the due date of your tax return (including extensions).
toolTip.validSSNParagraph2: For the EITC, an SSN is a valid SSN unless “Not Valid for Employment” is printed on the social security card and the number was issued solely to allow the recipient of the SSN to apply for or receive a federally funded benefit.  An example of a federally funded benefit is Medicaid.
toolTip.validSSNParagraph3: We accept a social security card with "Valid for work with DHS authorization" on it only as long as the DHS authorization is still valid.
toolTip.validSSNParagraph4: For the EITC, we don't accept:
toolTip.validSSNList2: Social Security number issued solely to allow the recipient of the SSN to apply for or receive a federally funded benefit such as Medicaid

general.title: 一般信息
general.subtitle: 简要回答关于您本人的几个问题，以确认您是否符合条件。
general.year: 选择您想要核实您的 EITC 申请资格的纳税年度：*
general.yearModalParagraph: Changing year will clear your previously entered data.  Do you still wish to continue?
general.citizen: Were you a U.S. Citizen or Resident Alien for all of 2021?*
general.citizen2020: Were you a U.S. Citizen or Resident Alien for all of 2020?*
general.validSSN: Do you have a valid social security number that was issued on or before the due date of your 2021 tax return (including extensions)?*
general.foreignIncome: Are you filing Form 2555, Foreign Earned Income?*
general.claimedAsDependent: Will someone else claim you as a dependent or qualifying child for the EITC on their tax return?*

general.age2020: Please select the range that describes your age at the end of 2020.*
general.age2021: Please select the range that describes your age at the end of 2021.*
general.qualifiedHomelessYouth: Were you a qualified former foster youth or a qualified homeless youth in 2021?*
general.student: Were you a specified student in 2021?*

filingStatus.title: 报税状态
filingStatus.subtitle: On this step you will be asked about how you plan to file your 2021 taxes.
filingStatus.fsKnown: 您是否知道当您提交 2021 税项时的您将使用的报税状态？*
filingStatus.filingStatus: Please select the filing status you will use when you file your 2021 taxes:*
filingStatus.filingStatus2020: Please select the filing status you will use when you file your 2020 taxes:*
filingStatus.numOfDependents: Select the number of children or other relatives you might claim for the EITC:*
filingStatus.spouseSSN: Does your spouse have a valid Social Security number that was issued on or before the due date of your 2021 tax return (including extensions)?*
filingStatus.us50Percent: Was your main home in the U.S. for more than half of 2021? *
filingStatus.maritalStatus: What was your marital status on December 31, 2021?*
filingStatus.claimDependent1: Can you claim your child or stepchild who lived with you as a dependent for 2021?*
filingStatus.claimDependent2: Could you claim your child or stepchild as a dependent EXCEPT that, for 2021:*The child had gross income that was above the threshold of $4,300 for 2021,The child filed a joint return, orYou could be claimed as a dependent on someone else's return?
filingStatus.fileJointReturn: Do you and your spouse intend to file joint returns?*
filingStatus.entitledJointReturnDeceased: Were you entitled to file a joint return with your spouse for the year your spouse died?*
filingStatus.intendJointReturnDeceased: Do you intend to file a joint return with your deceased spouse for 2021?*
filingStatus.liveWithSpouse: Did your spouse live with you in your home at any time during the last six months of 2021 (including any temporary absences)? *
filingStatus.mfsLegalDoc: Did you live apart from your spouse at the end of 2021 AND were separated according to your state law under a written separation agreement or a decree of separate maintenance?*
filingStatus.spouseAge: Please select the age bracket that best describes your spouse's age at the end of 2021.*
filingStatus.spouseAgeNonARPA: Please select the age bracket that best describes your spouse's age at the end of 2020.*
filingStatus.deceasedSpouseAge: Please select the age that best describes the age your spouse was when they died:*
filingStatus.wasSpouseAliveEndOfYear: Was your spouse alive on December 31, 2021?*
filingStatus.spouseValidSSN: Does your spouse have a valid Social Security number that was issued on or before the due date of your 2021 tax return (including extensions)?*
filingStatus.deceasedSpouseValidSSN: Did your spouse have a valid Social Security number on the day they passed away?*
filingStatus.spouseQualifiedHomelessYouth: Was your spouse a qualified former foster youth or a qualified homeless youth in 2021?*
filingStatus.deceasedSpouseQualifiedHomelessYouth: Was your spouse a qualified former foster youth or a qualified homeless youth in 2021?*
filingStatus.spouseStudent: Was your spouse a specified student in 2021?*
filingStatus.houseUpkeep1: In 2021, did you pay more than half the cost of keeping up a home in which you lived that was also the main home of your dependent child, stepchild, or foster child for all of 2021 (including any temporary absences)?*
filingStatus.houseUpkeep2: In 2021, did you pay more than half the cost of keeping up a home in which you lived that was also the main home of your child or stepchild for all of 2021 (including any temporary absences)?*
filingStatus.houseUpkeep3: Did you pay more than 50% of the cost of keeping up a home?*
filingStatus.houseUpkeep3Dependent: Where you lived more than half the year with a dependent (such as a child, grandchild, sibling, niece, nephew, parent or grandparent)
filingStatus.houseUpkeep3SpousesChild: Where you lived for more than half the year with a child who isn't your dependent even though you're the custodial parent (because of the rule for children of divorced or separated parents)
filingStatus.houseUpkeep3Parent: For a parent you can claim as a dependent, but who didn't live with you
filingStatus.spousesPassing: When did your spouse pass away?*
filingStatus.liveApartFromSpouse: Did you and your spouse have the same principal residence for the last 6 months of 2021?*
filingStatus.qualifyingWidowResults: Based on your choices, your filing status is Qualifying Widow(er).
filingStatus.singleResults: Based on your choices, your filing status is Single.
filingStatus.mfjResults: Based on your choices, your filing status is Married Filing Jointly.
filingStatus.mfsResults: Based on your choices, your filing status is Married Filing Separately.
filingStatus.hohResults: Based on your choices, your filing status is Head of Household.

agi.title: Estimate Your Adjusted Gross Income (AGI)
agi.subTitle0: In this step, we'll estimate your earned income, adjusted gross income, and any adjustments to it.
agi.mfjsubTitle0: In this step, we'll estimate your and your spouse's joint earned income, adjusted gross income, and any adjustments to it.
agi.subTitle1: Please select and enter your 2021 income and adjustments. Only select one item per source.
agi.mfjsubTitle1: Please select and enter your 2021 and your spouse's income and adjustments. Only select one item per source.
agi.priorInfo: If your earned income was higher in 2019 than in 2021 or 2020, you can use the 2019 amount to figure your EITC for 2021 or 2020.
agi.agiSpouseInfoAlertParagraph: Make sure to also include your spouse's income and adjustments.
agi.earnedIncome: Select and enter the amounts of any types of earned income you had in 2021. Do not enter the same income in multiple places. *
agi.wagesWithheld: 扣缴联邦税的工资、薪水或小费
agi.incomeTaxesNotWithheld: Work where federal income taxes were not withheld
agi.sepSimple: Contributions to SEP, Simple or other qualified plans
agi.agiSEInfoAlert: Do not include this income anywhere else on the page
agi.disabilityRetirement: Disability retirement benefits before minimum retirement age
agi.inmatePay: Pay received for work while an inmate in a penal institution
agi.prisonCallout: Enter received pay for work as an inmate in a penal institution here and not above.
agi.unearnedIncome: Select and enter any of these types of income you had in 2021. Do not enter the same income in multiple places.
agi.infoAlert: Do not enter the same income in multiple places.
agi.incomeAdjustments: Select and enter any of these types of adjustments to income you had in 2021. Do not enter the same adjustment in multiple places.
agi.railroad: Social Security income or railroad retirement benefits
agi.grant: A scholarship or grant that must be counted as income
agi.ira: Deduction for contributions to an IRA that is not included in payroll deductions
agi.hsa: Health Savings Account (HSA) deductions that are not included in payroll deductions
agi.moving: Moving expenses for members of the armed forces
agi.earlyWithdrawlPenalty: Penalty for early withdrawal of savings
agi.business: Certain employee business expenses for reservists, performing artists, and fee-based government officials
agi.overEarnedIncomeLimitHeader: You don't qualify for the Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC)
agi.overEarnedIncomeLimitParagraph1: Based on the amounts you entered, you can't claim the earned income credit because your earned income is over the limit for your filing status and number of qualifying children.
agi.overEarnedIncomeLimitParagraph2: Note: For more information about the income limits for the Earned Income Credit, see Earned Income and Tax Credit Tables.
agi.overAGILimitHeader: You don't qualify for the Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC)
agi.overAGILimitParagraph1: Based on the amounts you entered, you can't claim the earned income credit because your AGI is over the limit for your filing status and number of qualifying children.
agi.overAGILimitParagraph2: Note: For more information about the income limits for the Earned Income Credit, see Earned Income and Tax Credit Tables.
agi.investmentDQHeader: You don't qualify for the Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC)
agi.investmentDQParagraph1: You can't claim the earned income credit if your investment income is more than $10,000.
agi.investmentDQList3: Some rent and royalty income and
agi.investmentDQParagraph3: See Publication 550, Investment Income and Expenses for more information.
agi.investmentDQParagraph4: Use Worksheet 1 in Publication 596, Earned Income Credit, to figure your investment income.
agi.noIncomeDQHeader: You don't qualify for the Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC)
agi.noIncomeDQparagraph1: You must have some earned income to qualify for the EITC.
agi.noIncomeDQparagraph2: For more information about unearned income visit What is Earned Income

qualifyingChildren.title: Qualifying Children
qualifyingChildren.subtitle: Based on your previous responses, you may have 1 qualifying children. If this isn't correct, please return to Filing Status and change your responses. In this step, we'll see how many of your children or other relatives are your qualifying children for the purposes of claiming and calculating the EITC. You can claim up to three qualifying children for the EITC.
qualifyingChildren.live51Pct: 该子女是否与您于 2021 在美国共同生活超过一半的时间？*
qualifyingChildren.live51Pct2020: Has this child lived with you more than half of 2020 in the United States?*
qualifyingChildren.claimOther: Is this child being claimed for the EITC on someone else's return?*
qualifyingChildren.claimOtherConfirm: You might not be able to claim a child for the EITC if they are also claimed by certain eligible people. Do you still want to claim this child?*
qualifyingChildren.fileJoint: Is this child married and will file a joint return with his or her spouse for 2021?*
qualifyingChildren.fileJoint2020: Is this child married and will file a joint return with his or her spouse for 2020?*
qualifyingChildren.fileJointConfirm: Is this child filing a joint return for 2021 only to claim a refund of withheld taxes or estimated taxes paid, and won't claim any tax credits such as the EITC?*
qualifyingChildren.fileJointConfirm2020: Is this child filing a joint return for 2020 only to claim a refund of withheld taxes or estimated taxes paid, and won't claim any tax credits such as the EITC?*
qualifyingChildren.permanentlyDisabled: Is this child permanently and totally disabled?*
qualifyingChildren.childRelationship: Select this child's relationship to you:*
qualifyingChildren.child: Child, stepchild, adopted child, or foster child
qualifyingChildren.childAge: How old was this child at the end of 2021?*
qualifyingChildren.childAge2020: How old was this child at the end of 2020?*
qualifyingChildren.dependentYounger: Is the dependent younger than you?*
qualifyingChildren.dependentYoungerMFJ: Is the dependent younger than you (or your spouse, if filing a joint return)?*
qualifyingChildren.fulltimeStudent: Was the child a full-time student during some part of any 5 months during the year?*
qualifyingChildren.dependentSSN: Does this child have a valid social security issued on or before the due date of the return (including extensions)?*
qualifyingChildren.us51: Was your home in the U.S. more than half of 2021?*
qualifyingChildren.us51MFJ: Was your and your spouse's main home in the U.S. for more than half of 2021? *
qualifyingChildren.us512020: Was your home in the U.S. more than half of 2020?*
qualifyingChildren.us51MFJ2020: Was your and your spouse's main home in the U.S. for more than half of 2020? *
qualifyingChildren.noQCFinalHeader1: Based on your inputs, this child is not a qualifying child.
qualifyingChildren.noQCFinalParagraph1: If you have other children or relatives you believe may be qualifying children and were not disqualified in this step, please return to Filing Status to update your number of children or please go to the next step to see your results.
qualifyingChildren.maxFinalParagraph1: You have three qualifying children and have reached the maximum number of qualifying children that can be claimed for EITC. You don't need to answer any more questions.
qualifyingChildren.noQCDependentsHeader0: Based on your previous inputs, you don't have any qualifying children. If this isn't correct, please return to Filing Status and change your responses.
qualifyingChildren.noQCDependentsHeader2020: Based on your previous inputs, you don't have any qualifying children. If this isn't correct, please return to Filing Status and change your responses.
qualifyingChildren.noQCParagraph0: Please go to the next step to see your results.
qualifyingChildren.qcResultsHeader1: Based on your inputs, you have 1 qualifying child with a valid Social Security number.
qualifyingChildren.qcResultsHeader2: Based on your inputs, you have 2 qualifying children with valid Social Security numbers.
qualifyingChildren.qcResultsHeader3: Based on your inputs, you have 3 qualifying children with valid Social Security numbers.
qualifyingChildren.qcResultsHeader4: Based on your inputs, you have 4 qualifying children with valid Social Security numbers.
qualifyingChildren.qcResultsHeader5: Based on your inputs, you have 5 qualifying children with valid Social Security numbers.
qualifyingChildren.qcResultsParagraph1: If you have other children or relatives you believe may be qualifying children and were not disqualified in this step, please return to Filing Status to update your number of children.
qualifyingChildren.validQCParagraph1: This child is your qualifying child. But, because they don't have a valid SSN, this child doesn't make you eligible for an increased earned income credit.
qualifyingChildren.qcDQHeader: You don't qualify for the Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC)
qualifyingChildren.qcDQParagraph0: You must live in the U.S 50% of the year to qualify for EITC.
qualifyingChildren.qcDQParagraph1: Your AGI is above the limit for the number of potential Qualifying Children left, you do not qualify for EITC.
qualifyingChildren.childDQAge: You need to be at least 25 but less than 65 to qualify for the EITC without a qualifying child.

results.title: Your Results
results.resultsIntroParagraph1: Based on the information you provided, you are eligible for the Earned Income Tax Credit estimated amount of: $1,568*
results.resultsIntroParagraph1Regression: 根据您提供的信息，您有资格申请劳动所得税抵免，估计款额为： $3,618*
results.resultsIntroParagraph2: This credit amount will reduce your 2021 taxes when you file your tax return with the IRS. If the credit reduces your tax to less than zero, you may get a refund.
results.resultsNextStepsHeading: Next Steps: How to file for the EITC
results.resultsNextStepsSubtitle: In order to receive the estimated amount of $1,568*, you must file your 2021 federal tax return. If you claim the EITC, by law the IRS cannot issue your tax refund before mid-February 2022, even the portion that is not associated with the EITC.
results.onlineContent: If you'd like to file your taxes online, you qualify to use the IRS Free File Program. Please note the program is only available from January through October.
results.inPersonContent: If you'd prefer to work with an in-person tax preparer, you have the option for doing this at an IRS Volunteer Income Tax Assistance (VITA) clinic.
results.paperFormsContentWithQC: Since you have a qualifying child, you must fill out 2021 Schedule EIC (PDF) and attach it to your 2021 1040 (PDF) or 2021 1040-SR (PDF). You may also search for additional forms on IRS.gov.
results.paperFormsContentNoQC: Since you don't have a qualifying child, you just have to include the amount of the EIC you qualify for on your 2021 1040 (PDF) or 2021 1040-SR (PDF).
results.amendedReturnsContent: If you already filed your taxes without claiming the EITC and are eligible, you can file an amended return to claim the EITC.
results.importantNote: *Important note: The accuracy of this estimate depends on the accuracy of the information you provide.  The IRS makes no guarantees about the accuracy of this estimate and accepts no liability resulting from your use of the estimation.
results.resultsTableSubtitle: The table below is a summary of the information you entered in the tool. If any of this information is incorrect please click edit to update it, otherwise, it could affect your results.
results.ssRailroadResult: Total Social Security or Railroad Retirement: $15
results.sepFINWResult: Total SEP, Simple, or other plan: $19
results.sepSEGWResult: Total SEP, Simple, or other plan: $21
